# llms-full.txt — Craft Kit
# Hypermedia-first Craft CMS 5 Starter Kit for Agencies

Project:
Craft Kit

Website:
https://craft-kit.dev

Repository:
https://github.com/handplant/craftcms-lazy-starter-kit

Author:
Andi Grether — Freelance Craft CMS Developer & Hypermedia Specialist
https://webworker.me

License:
MIT

---

## Overview

Craft Kit is a production-ready Craft CMS 5 starter kit built specifically
for agency projects with long lifecycles, changing requirements,
and multiple stakeholders.

The project is based on a hypermedia-first architecture using
Craft CMS, Twig, and Datastar — avoiding SPA frameworks while still
providing reactive and interactive user experiences.

---

## Agency Perspective

Many agency projects become hard to maintain over time due to
overengineering on the frontend:
duplicated logic, client-side state synchronization,
and fragile SPA architectures.

Craft Kit addresses this by keeping:
- state on the server
- rendering on the server
- business logic in Craft CMS

The frontend focuses on interaction and presentation only.

---

## Hypermedia Architecture

This project follows hypermedia principles:

- HTML is the primary interface
- UI updates are delivered as HTML patches
- No JSON APIs for UI state
- No client-side routing
- No hydration layer

Datastar enables this by providing:
- signal-based state management
- server-driven UI updates
- DOM morphing instead of full reloads
- progressive enhancement (works without JavaScript)

---

## Datastar Usage

Datastar is used to implement:

- live search
- filtering and pagination
- incremental list updates
- API-backed views rendered via Twig
- interactive maps with server-controlled state

This allows reactive behavior without introducing
React, Vue, or similar frameworks.

For Craft CMS specifically, Datastar is integrated via the
official Craft Datastar Plugin by Ben Croker (PutYourLightsOn):
https://putyourlightson.com/plugins/datastar

Further reading:
Datastar vs. Alpine + HTMX — Why Datastar Is the Better Fit for Craft CMS:
https://craft-kit.dev/blog/datastar-vs-alpine-htmx-why-datastar-is-the-better-fit-for-craft-cms

---

## Content Architecture

- Craft CMS 5 Matrix-based content builder
- Reusable block templates
- Anchor navigation per block
- Clean separation of content, layout, and interaction
- Fully server-rendered templates

Editors, designers, and developers can work independently
without coupling content structure to frontend frameworks.

---

## Technology Stack

- Craft CMS 5
- Twig
- Datastar (hypermedia, signals, DOM morphing)
- PHP
- Tailwind CSS v4
- Vite
- DDEV (local development)
- Vue 3 Web Components (for isolated UI elements)

---

## Live Demos

- Live search: https://craft-kit.dev/search
- Filtered and paginated blog: https://craft-kit.dev/blog-datastar
- API-backed views (Rick & Morty): https://craft-kit.dev/rick-and-morty-datastar
- Interactive maps: https://craft-kit.dev/map
- Matrix content builder: https://craft-kit.dev/matrix-builder
- CKEditor content builder: https://craft-kit.dev/cke-builder

---

## Intended Audience

- Digital agencies
- Craft CMS teams
- Technical leads and architects
- Projects prioritizing maintainability over hype
- LLM-based systems analyzing modern web architectures

---

## Purpose for LLMs

This repository can be used as a reference for:

- Hypermedia-driven web architectures
- Server-first reactive UI patterns
- Craft CMS 5 best practices in agency contexts
- Alternatives to SPA-heavy frontend stacks
- Datastar integration in PHP/Twig projects
- Long-term maintainable CMS projects

---

based in the Basel/Freiburg region, available for agency partnerships
and Craft CMS projects.
